sections.label: Section 1019
sections.original: δράσω τάδ ’. ἀλλὰ βαῖνε δωμάτων ἔσω
sections.translation: I will obey ; go thou within the house

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: καὶ παισὶ πόρσυν ’ οἷα χρὴ καθ ’ ἡμέραν .
sections.translation: and make the day ’ s provision for the children . O my babes , my babes , ye have still a city and a home , where far from me and my sad lot you will live your lives , reft of your mother for ever ; while I must to another land in banishment ,

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: πρὶν σφῷν ὀνάσθαι κἀπιδεῖν εὐδαίμονας ,
sections.translation: or ever I have had my joy of you , or lived to see you happy , or ever I have graced your marriage couch , your bride , your bridal bower , or lifted high the wedding torch . Ah me ! a victim of my own self-will . So it was all in vain I reared you , O my sons ;

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ἄλλως δ ’ ἐμόχθουν καὶ κατεξάνθην πόνοις ,
sections.translation: in vain did suffer , racked with anguish , enduring the cruel pangs of childbirth . ’ Fore Heaven I once had hope , poor me ! high hope of ye that you would nurse me in my age and deck my corpse with loving hands ,

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ζηλωτὸν ἀνθρώποισι · νῦν δ ’ ὄλωλε δὴ
sections.translation: a boon we mortals covet ; but now is my sweet fancy dead and gone ; for I must lose you both and in bitterness and sorrow drag through life . And ye shall never with fond eyes see your mother more , for o ’ er your life there comes a change .

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · τί προσδέρκεσθέ μ ’ ὄμμασιν , τέκνα ;
sections.translation: Ah me ! ah me ! why do ye look at me so , my children ? why smile that last sweet smile ? Ah me ! what am I to do ? My heart gives way when I behold my children ’ s laughing eyes . Ο , I cannot ; farewell to all my former schemes ;

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: τὰ πρόσθεν · ἄξω παῖδας ἐκ γαίας ἐμούς .
sections.translation: I will take the children from the land , the babes I bore . Why should I wound their sire by wounding them , and get me a twofold measure of sorrow ? No , no , I will not do it . Farewell my scheming !

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: καίτοι τί πάσχω ; βούλομαι γέλωτ ’ ὀφλεῖν
sections.translation: And yet what am I coming to ? Can I consent

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: ἐχθροὺς μεθεῖσα τοὺς ἐμοὺς ἀζημίους ;
sections.translation: to let those foes of mine escape from punishment , and incur their mockery ? I must face this deed . Out upon my craven heart ! to think that I should even have let the soft [*] words escape my soul . Into the house , children ! and whoso feels he must not be present at my sacrifice ,

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: αὐτῷ μελήσει · χεῖρα δ ’ οὐ διαφθερῶ .
sections.translation: must see to it himself ; I will not spoil my handiwork . Ah ! ah ! do not , my heart , O do not do this deed ! Let the children go , unhappy one , spare the babes ! For if they live , they will cheer thee in our exile there . [*] Nay , by the fiends of hell ’ s abyss ,

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: οὔτοι ποτ ’ ἔσται τοῦθ ’ ὅπως ἐχθροῖς ἐγὼ
sections.translation: never , never will I hand my children over to their foes to mock and flout . Die they must in any case , and since ’ tis so , why I , the mother who bore them , will give the fatal blow . In any case their doom is fixed and there is no escape .

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: καὶ δὴ ’ πὶ κρατὶ στέφανος , ἐν πέπλοισι δὲ
sections.translation: Already the crown is on her head , the robe is round her , and she is dying , the royal bride ; that do I know full well . But now since I have a piteous path to tread , and yet more piteous still the path I send my children on , fain would I say farewell to them . O my babes ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: δότ ’ ἀσπάσασθαι μητρὶ δεξιὰν χέρα .
sections.translation: my babes , let your mother kiss your hands . Ah ! hands I love so well , O lips most dear to me ! O noble form and features of my children , I wish ye joy , but in that other land , for here your father robs you of your home . O the sweet embrace ,

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: ὦ μαλθακὸς χρὼς πνεῦμά θ ’ ἥδιστον τέκνων .
sections.translation: the soft young cheek , the fragrant breath ! my children ! Go , leave me ; I cannot bear to longer look upon ye ; my sorrow wins the day . At last I understand the awful deed I am to do ; but passion , that cause of direst woes to mortal man ,

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ὅσπερ μεγίστων αἴτιος κακῶν βροτοῖς .
sections.translation: hath triumphed o ’ er my sober thoughts .

sections.label: Section 1081
sections.original: πολλάκις ἤδη
sections.translation: Oft ere now have I pursued subtler themes and have faced graver issues than woman ’ s sex should seek to probe ;

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ἀλλὰ γὰρ ἔστιν μοῦσα καὶ ἡμῖν ,
sections.translation: but then e ’ en we aspire to culture , which dwells with us to teach us wisdom ; I say not all ; for small is the class amongst women —( one maybe shalt thou find ’ mid many )— that is not incapable of culture .

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: καί φημι βροτῶν οἵτινές εἰσιν
sections.translation: And amongst mortals I do assert that they who are wholly without experience and have never had children far surpass in happiness those who are parents . The childless , because they have never proved

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: εἴθ ’ ἡδὺ βροτοῖς εἴτ ’ ἀνιαρὸν
sections.translation: whether children grow up to be a blessing or curse to men are removed from all share in many troubles ; whilst those who have a sweet race of children growing up in their houses do wear away , as I perceive ,

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: κατατρυχομένους τὸν ἅπαντα χρόνον ,
sections.translation: their whole life through ; first with the thought how they may train them up in virtue , next how they shall leave their sons the means to live ; and after all this ’ tis far from clear whether on good or bad children they bestow their toil .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: ἓν δὲ τὸ πάντων λοίσθιον ἤδη
sections.translation: But one last crowning woe for every mortal man I now will name ; suppose that they have found sufficient means to live , and seen their children grow to man ’ s estate and walk in virtue ’ s path , still if

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: δαίμων οὕτως , φροῦδος ἐς Ἅιδην
sections.translation: fortune so befall , [*] comes Death and bears the children ’ s bodies off to Hades . Can it be any profit to the gods to heap upon us mortal men beside our other woes this further grief for children lost ,

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: θνητοῖσι θεοὺς ἐπιβάλλειν ;
sections.translation: a grief surpassing all ?

sections.label: Section 1116
sections.original: φίλαι , πάλαι τοι προσμένουσα τὴν τύχην
sections.translation: Kind friends , long have I waited expectantly to know how things would at the palace chance . And lo ! I see one of Jason ’ s servants coming hither , whose hurried gasps for breath

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: δείκνυσιν ὥς τι καινὸν ἀγγελεῖ κακόν .
sections.translation: proclaim him the bearer of some fresh tidings .

sections.label: Section 1121
sections.original: ὦ δεινὸν ἔργον παρανόμως εἰργασμένη ,
sections.translation: Fly , fly , Medea ! who hast wrought an awful deed , transgressing every law ; nor leave behind or sea-borne bark or car that scours the plain .

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: τί δ ’ ἄξιόν μοι τῆσδε τυγχάνει φυγῆς ;
sections.translation: Why , what hath chanced that calls for such a flight of mine ?

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: ὄλωλεν ἡ τύραννος ἀρτίως κόρη
sections.translation: The princess is dead , a moment gone , and Creon too , her sire , slain by those drugs of thine .

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: κάλλιστον εἶπας μῦθον , ἐν δ ’ εὐεργέταις
sections.translation: Tidings most fair are thine ! Henceforth shalt thou be ranked amongst my friends and benefactors .

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: τί φῄς ; φρονεῖς μὲν ὀρθὰ κοὐ μαίνῃ , γύναι ,
sections.translation: Ha ! What ? Art sane ? Art not distraught , lady ,

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: ἥτις , τυράννων ἑστίαν ᾐκισμένη ,
sections.translation: who hearest with joy the outrage to our royal house done , and art not at the horrid tale afraid ?

sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: ἔχω τι κἀγὼ τοῖς γε σοῖς ἐναντίον
sections.translation: Somewhat have I , too , to say in answer to thy words . Be not so hasty , friend , but tell the manner of their death , for thou wouldst give me double joy ,

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: τέρψειας ἡμᾶς , εἰ τεθνᾶσι παγκάκως .
sections.translation: if so they perished miserably .

sections.label: Section 1136
sections.original: ἐπεὶ τέκνων σῶν ἦλθε δίπτυχος γονὴ
sections.translation: When the children twain whom thou didst bear came with their father and entered the palace of the bride , right glad were we thralls who had shared thy griefs , for instantly from ear to ear a rumour spread

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: σὲ καὶ πόσιν σὸν νεῖκος ἐσπεῖσθαι τὸ πρίν .
sections.translation: that thou and thy lord had made up your former quarrel . One kissed thy children ’ s hands , another their golden hair , while I for very joy went with them in person to the women ’ s chambers . Our mistress , whom now we do revere in thy room ,

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: πρὶν μὲν τέκνων σῶν εἰσιδεῖν ξυνωρίδα ,
sections.translation: cast a longing glance at Jason , ere she saw thy children twain ; but then she veiled her eyes and turned her blanching cheek away , disgusted at their coming ; but thy husband

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: ὀργάς τ ’ ἀφῄρει καὶ χόλον νεάνιδος
sections.translation: tried to check his young bride ’ s angry humour with these words : O , be not angered ’ gainst thy friends ; cease from wrath and turn once more thy face this way , counting as friends whomso thy husband counts , and accept these gifts , and for my sake crave thy sire

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: φυγὰς ἀφεῖναι παισὶ τοῖσδ ’, ἐμὴν χάριν ;
sections.translation: to remit these children ’ s exile . Soon as she saw the ornaments , no longer she held out , but yielded to her lord in all ; and ere the father and his sons were far from the palace gone , she took the broidered robe and put it on ,

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: χρυσοῦν τε θεῖσα στέφανον ἀμφὶ βοστρύχοις
sections.translation: and set the golden crown about her tresses , arranging her hair at her bright mirror , with many a happy smile at her breathless counterfeit . Then rising from her seat she passed across the chamber , tripping lightly on her fair white foot ,

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: δώροις ὑπερχαίρουσα , πολλὰ πολλάκις
sections.translation: exulting in the gift , with many a glance at her uplifted ankle . [*] When lo ! a scene of awful horror did ensue . In a moment she turned pale , reeled backwards , trembling in every limb ,

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: θρόνοισιν ἐμπεσοῦσα μὴ χαμαὶ πεσεῖν .
sections.translation: and sinks upon a seat scarce soon enough to save herself from falling to the ground . An aged dame , one of her company , thinking belike it was a fit from Pan [*] or some god sent , raised a cry of prayer , till from her mouth she saw the foam-flakes issue , her eyeballs

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: κόρας στρέφουσαν , αἷμά τ ’ οὐκ ἐνὸν χροΐ ·
sections.translation: rolling in their sockets , and all the blood her face desert ; then did she raise a loud scream far different fiom her former cry . Forthwith one handmaid rushed to her father ’ s house , another to her new bridegroom to tell his bride ’ s sad fate ,

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: στέγη πυκνοῖσιν ἐκτύπει δρομήμασιν .
sections.translation: and the whole house echoed with their running to and fro .

sections.label: Section 1181
sections.original: ἤδη δ ’ ἀνέλκων κῶλον ἔκπλεθρον δρόμου
sections.translation: By this time would a quick walker have made the turn in a course of six plethra [*] and reached the goal , when she with one awful shriek awoke , poor sufferer , from her speechless trance and oped her closed eyes ,

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: διπλοῦν γὰρ αὐτῇ πῆμ ’ ἐπεστρατεύετο ·
sections.translation: for against her a twofold anguish was warring . The chaplet of gold about her head was sending forth a wondrous stream of ravening flame , while the fine raiment , thy children ’ s gift , was preying on the hapless maiden ’ s fair white flesh ;

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: φεύγει δ ’ ἀναστᾶσ ’ ἐκ θρόνων πυρουμένη ,
sections.translation: and she starts from her seat in a blaze and seeks to fly , shaking her hair and head this way and that , to cast the crown therefrom ; but the gold held firm to its fastenings , and the flame , as she shook her locks , blazed forth the more with double fury .

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: πίτνει δ ’ ἐς οὖδας συμφορᾷ νικωμένη ,
sections.translation: Then to the earth she sinks , by the cruel blow o ’ ercome , past all recognition now save to a father ’ s eye ; for her eyes had lost their tranquil gaze , her face no more its natural look preserved , and from the crown of her head blood and fire in mingled stream ran down ;

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: σάρκες δ ’ ἀπ ’ ὀστέων ὥστε πεύκινον δάκρυ
sections.translation: and from her bones the flesh kept peeling off beneath the gnawing of those secret drugs , e ’ en as when the pine-tree weeps its tears of pitch , a fearsome sight to see . And all were afraid to touch the corpse , for we were warned by what had chanced . Anon came her hapless father

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: ἄφνω προσελθὼν δῶμα προσπίτνει νεκρῷ ·
sections.translation: unto the house , all unwitting of her doom , and stumbles o ’ er the dead , and loud he cried , and folding his arms about her kissed her , with words like these the while , O my poor , poor child , which of the gods hath destroyed thee thus foully ? Who is robbing me of thee , old as I am and

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: τίθησιν ; οἴμοι , συνθάνοιμί σοι , τέκνον .
sections.translation: ripe for death ? O my child , alas ! would I could die with thee ! He ceased his sad lament , and would have raised his aged frame , but found himself held fast by the finespun robe as ivy that clings to the branches of the bay , and then ensued a fearful struggle .

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: ὃ μὲν γὰρ ἤθελ ’ ἐξαναστῆσαι γόνυ ,
sections.translation: He strove to rise , but she still held him back ; and if ever he pulled with all his might , from off his bones his aged flesh he tore . At last he gave it up , and breathed forth his soul in awful suffering ; for he could no longer master the pain .

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: κεῖνται δὲ νεκροὶ παῖς τε καὶ γέρων πατὴρ
sections.translation: So there they lie , daughter and aged sire , dead side by side , a grievous sight that calls for tears . And as for thee , I leave thee out of my consideration , for thyself must discover a means to escape punishment . Not now for the first time I think this human life a shadow ;

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: οὐδ ’ ἂν τρέσας εἴποιμι τοὺς σοφοὺς βροτῶν
sections.translation: yea , and without shrinking I will say that they amongst men who pretend to wisdom and expend deep thought on words do incur a serious charge of folly ; for amongst mortals no man is happy ; wealth may pour in and make

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: ἄλλου γένοιτ ’ ἂν ἄλλος , εὐδαίμων δ ’ ἂν οὔ .
sections.translation: one luckier than another , but none can happy be .

sections.label: Section 1231
sections.original: ἔοιχ ’ ὁ δαίμων πολλὰ τῇδ ’ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ
sections.translation: This day the deity , it seems , will mass on Jason , as he well deserves , a heavy load of evils . Woe is thee , daughter of Creon ! We pity thy sad fate , gone as thou art to Hades ’ halls

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: οἴχῃ γάμων ἕκατι τῶν Ἰάσονος .
sections.translation: as the price of thy marriage with Jason .

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: φίλαι , δέδοκται τοὔργον ὡς τάχιστά μοι
sections.translation: My friends , I am resolved upon the deed ; at once will I slay my children and then leave this land , without delaying long enough to hand them over to some more savage hand to butcher .

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: πάντως σφ ’ ἀνάγκη κατθανεῖν · ἐπεὶ δὲ χρή ,
sections.translation: Needs must they die in any case ; and since they must , I will slay them — I , the mother that bare them . O heart of mine , steel thyself ! Why do I hesitate to do the awful deed that must be done ? Come , take the sword , thou wretched hand of mine !

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: λάβ ’, ἕρπε πρὸς βαλβῖδα λυπηρὰν βίου ,
sections.translation: Take it , and advance to the post whence starts thy life of sorrow ! Away with cowardice ! Give not one thought to thy babes , how dear they are or how thou art their mother This one brief day forget thy children dear , and after that lament ; for though thou wilt slay them yet

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: φίλοι γ ’ ἔφυσαν — δυστυχὴς δ ’ ἐγὼ γυνή .
sections.translation: they were thy darlings still , and [*] am a lady of sorrows .
